original: David I becomes King of Scots
Treaty of St Truiden: anti-French Trapdoors and Bourgondisch covenant
Battle of Bicocca: a combined French and Venetian force is decisively defeated by a Spanish-Imperial and Papal army
Re-founding of the city of Bogotá, New Granada (now Colombia), by Nikolaus Federmann and Sebastián de Belalcázar.
First Spanish settlement in the Philippines is founded in Cebu City
Peace of Beaulieu and Paix de Monsieur
Tirso de Molina's "Bellaco Sois, Gomez" premieres in Madrid
Battle of Carbisdale: Royalist army under the Marquess of Montrose invades mainland Scotland from Orkney and is defeated by a Covenanter army
Netherlands and France sign a military agreement
Frederick Augustus I "the Strong" becomes the monarch of Saxony
Cornelis van Steenoven elected seventh Old Catholic Archbishop of Utrecht
British Parliament passes the Tea Act (Boston won't like this)
US Marines attack shores of Tripoli
Americans under General Zebulon Pike capture York (now Toronto) from British regulars, militia and Ojibwe natives forcing a British withdrawal to Kingston; Pike is killed
Zoological Gardens at Regent's Park in London opens
Fire destroys half of Charleston
Imakita Kosen, 1st Zen teacher of D.T. Suzuki, found the awakening
Establishment of Jewish congregations in Lower Austria prohibited
"Pomona" sinks in North Atlantic drowning all 400 aboard
West Virginia secedes from Virginia after Virginia secedes from Union (US Civil War)
Battle of Streight's Raid: Tuscumbia to Cedar Bluff, AL
Cornell University (Ithaca NY) is chartered

Steamboat "SS Sultana" explodes in the Mississippi River, killing up to 1,800 of the 2,427 passengers in the greatest maritime disaster in United States history; most are paroled Union POWs on their way home.
White League, Paramilitary white supremacist organization, forms
New York Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children (NYSPCC), the world's first such agency is incorporated
Pogroms against Russian Jews start in Elisabethgrad
French troops under Capt Archinard occupy Oussebougou, West Sudan
Richard "King Dick" Seddon succeeds John Balance as premier of New Zealand and leader of Liberal Party
Grant's Tomb (famed of song & legend) dedicated
Foundation of the Phonogrammarchiv of the Austrian Academy of Science, the world's first research sound archive (added to UNESCO's Memory of the World 1999)
1st Highlander (Yankee) shut-out, Philadelphia A's win 6-0
Long Island's Jamaica Race Track opens
World Exposition opens in Liège
IV Summer (Modern) Olympic Games open in London
Sultan of Turkey Abdul Hamid II is overthrown
Belgian parliament rejects socialist motion for general voting rights
Following the resignation and death of William P. Frye, a compromise is reached to rotate the office of President pro tempore (for the time being) of the United States Senate
Relief laws in Netherlands replace those of 1854
Honduras becomes a signatory to the Buenos Aires copyright treaty
The British renew their assault on the Irish Volunteer position in Mount Street; shelling also sets the buildings on fire
Giants' 9-0 winning start & Dodgers' 0-9 losing streak are stopped
Pogrom leader Petljoera declares Ukraine Independence
Sweden takes the figure skating double at the Antwerp Olympics when Gillis Grafström wins his first of 3 consecutive men's gold medals; Magda Julin earlier wins the women's singles event
Hadjememaar, [Corn de Gelder] elected in Amsterdam
Fritz Lang's "Dr Mabuse, der Spieler" premieres in Berlin
Yakut ASSR formed in Russian SFSR
Antwerp soccer tie Belgium-Netherlands 1-1
Carabineros de Chile (Chilean national police force and gendarmery) created
White Sox 1st baseman Bud Clancy didn't handle ball at all in a 9 inning game vs St Louis Browns
100°F (38°C), Pahala, Hawaii (state record)
Jessop & Son department store in Nottingham, England, acquired by John Lewis Partnership. The partnership's first shop outside London.
Karl Jansky reports reception of cosmic radio signal in Washington, D.C.
Brussels World Exposition opens
Yanks pull a 1st inning triple-play & beat Philadelphia A's 9-8
1st US social security payment made
German troops occupy Athens, Greece
Belgian Jews are forced to wear stars
Tornado destroys Pryor, Oklahoma, killing 100, injuring 300
Soviet Union breaks contact with Polish government exiled in London
Boston Brave Jim Tobin no-hits Bkln Dodgers, 2-0
2nd Republic of Austria forms
US 5th army enters Genoa
World War II: The Völkischer Beobachter, the newspaper of the Nazi Party, ceases publication
1st radar installed aboard a commercial ship
Arab legion attacks Gesher bridge on Jordan River
South Africa passes Group Areas Act segregating races
1st general elections in British Guiana, won by the People's Progressive Party with Cheddi Jagan first person of Indian descent to be elected head of government outside South Asia
Wrestler Freddie Blassie coins term "Pencil neck geek"
"Today" show goes abroard 1st time (Paris France)
The last Canadian missionary leaves the People's Republic of China.
1st atomic powered electric-drive submarine launched (Tullibee)
Togo (formerly French Togo) declares independence from French administration
NASA launches Explorer 11 into Earth orbit to study gamma rays
NFL officially recognizes Hall of Fame in Canton, Ohio
Sierra Leone declares independence from the United Kingdom
Arnold Wesker's "Chips with Everything" premieres in London
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Christmas Island
"Jopie" Pengel forms government in Suriname
Robert C. Duncan and Norma L. Baker are granted a patent for the "Pampers" disposable diaper on behalf of Procter & Gamble
Expo 67 opens in Montreal, Canada, featuring a giant Biosphere designed by Buckminster Fuller
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Baltimore Orioles pitcher Tom Phoebus no-hits Boston, 6-0
Congress of Political Party Radicals (PPR) forms in Netherlands
The Northern Ireland Civil Rights Association (NICRA) hold a rally to protest the banning of a Republican Easter parade
Apollo 16 returns to Earth after lunar exploration, splashing down in the Pacific Ocean 350 km (189 nmi) southeast of the island of Kiritimati
NYC Mayor John Lindsey appeals thatJohn Lennonnot be deported
KC Royals pitcher Steve Busby no-hits Detroit Tigers, 3-0
Pan Am 707 crashes into mountains of Bali, killing 107
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Arabic Monetary Fund established in Abu Dhabi
Bloody riots in Soweto, South Africa
HCC, Hobby Computer Club, forms in Netherlands
Afghanistan revolution (National Day), pro-Russian military coup
Cooling tower collapses at a coal-fired power plant at Willow Island, West Virginia, kills 51
First female soccer official is hired by the NASL
Xerox PARC introduces the computer mouse
NFL Draft: University of Texas defensive end Kenneth Sims first pick by New England Patriots
Cleveland Indians beat Detroit Tigers, 8-4, in 19 innings
Over 70 inches of snow falls on Red Lake Montana
Captain Midnight (John R MacDougall) hacks into and interrupts HBO
Beijing students take over Tiananmen Square in China
Hurricane in Bangladesh kills 500
Mandatory seatbelt law goes into effect in Italy
50th annual Barbershop Quartet Singing Convention held in Michigan
Villanova's women set a 6,000 m relay world record of 17:18:10
Firestone World Bowling Tournament of Champions won by David Ozio
Betty Boothroyd becomes the first woman to be elected Speaker of the British House of Commons in its 700-year history.
The Federal Republic of Yugoslavia, comprising Serbia and Montenegro, is proclaimed
Afghan Antonov AN-32 crashes at Tashqurgan, kills 76
7th longest NHL game at 125 min 43 sec: Buffalo Sabres beat New Jersey Devils 1-0 on a goal by Dave Hannan
Freedom Day: Day 2 of South Africa's first multi-racial elections and the day most people vote, with some queuing for 10 hours
Graeme Obree bicycles world record time (52,713 km)
Twins righty Scott Erickson no-hits Brewers 6-0
Brunswick World Tournament of Champions won by Dave D'Entremont
"Stanley" closes at Circle in the Square Theater, NYC
Nevada State Museum discovers the oldest mummy in North America - on the shelves of their own museum, after the Spirit Cave Mummy radiocarbon dates to over 9,400 years old
The last successful telemetry from the NASA space probe Pioneer 10
The superjumbo jet aircraft Airbus A380 makes its first flight from Toulouse, France
Construction begins on the Freedom Tower for the new World Trade Center in New York City
Estonian authorities remove the Bronze Soldier, a Soviet Red Army war memorial in Tallinn, amid political controversy with Russia
Deadliest day of the 2011 super outbreak of tornadoes, the largest tornado outbreak in United States history
Mummified remains of American actress Yvette Vickers discovered by a neighbor at her home; she had not been seen since 2010
Four explosions in Dnipropetrovsk, Ukraine, kill 27 people
10 people are killed and 25 are injured after a bomb attack in Karachi, Pakistan
NFL Draft: Texas A&M defensive end Myles Garrett first pick by Cleveland Browns
Mass protests across Spain after 5 men convicted of sexual abuse but not rape of teenage girl during Running of the Bulls festival in Pamplona
More than 40 Tuareg killed over two days by suspected jihadists in Menaka region, Mali
Shooter opens fire in a synagogue in Poway, California, killing one and injuring three
US Defense Department declassifies and releases videos of unidentified "aerial phenomena" from 2004 and 2015
Pfizer announces it is working on a new antiviral therapy to treat COVID-19 at its onset and prevent hospitalization
Italian women footballers finally turn professional after Italian Football Federation changes status of top division (takes effect 1 July) and ends capped amateur salaries
US CDC report finds that more than half of people (57.7%) had antibodies for COVID-19 by February 2022, with 75.2% of children and teenagers
Findings from the Zoonomia Project published  - where genomes of 240 mammals collected and compared. Reveals 10.7% of the human genome identical to almost all species.
NFL Draft: Alabama quarterback Bryce Young first pick by the Carolina Panthers
Pakistani architect Yasmeen Lari  awarded Royal Institute of British Architects' Royal Gold Medal for her humanitarian work providing structures for Pakistanis' marginalized communities
Iraq's parliament criminalizes same-sex relationships, passing a law that allows for up to 15 years in prison
Liverpool wins the 2024/25 English Premier League title against Tottenham (5-1)
Battle of Dunbar: The Scots are defeated byEdward Iof England
PopeJulius IIexcommunicates the Republic of Venice, and endorses his allies to attack
Duel of the Mignons claims the lives of two favorites ofHenry III of Franceand two favorites of Henry I, Duke of Guise
On the verge of defeat, KingCharles Iflees Oxford for a Scottish army camp close to Southwell near Newark-on-Trent
Blind and impoverished, English poetJohn Miltonsells the copyright of "Paradise Lost" for £10
First performance ofGeorge Frideric Handel's "Music For The Royal Fireworks" in Green Park, London
Ludwig van Beethovencomposes his famous piano piece "Für Elise"
Confederate GeneralRobert E. Leeorders ColonelThomas "Stonewall" Jacksonto take command of Harpers Ferry, Virginia

Charles Gounod's opera "Romeo et Juliette" is premieres at the Théâtre Lyrique, Paris, France
Jules Massenet's opera "Le Roi de Lahore" (The King of Lahore) premieres at the Palais Garnier, Paris, France
Rutherford B. Hayesremoves Federal troops from Louisiana, Reconstruction ends
Louis BothaandJ. B. M. Hertzogestablish the moderately nationalist South African Party promising equality of Britons and Boers
Indian passive resistance in South Africa is suspended when GeneralJan Smutsenters into negotiations withMahatma Gandhi
Counterattack launched by Turkish forces under the command ofMustafa Kemal Atatürkagainst allied troops
In the Giants' 9-8 win over Phillies,Mel Ott, 17, 1st appearance
Adolf Hitlerauthorizes creation of Ministry of Aviation, in part to revive the German Luftwaffe, under ReichsmarshallHermann Goering
Himmlerorders establishment of Auschwitz Concentration Camp
Witold Pileckiescapes from Auschwitz after having voluntarily been imprisoned there to gain information about the Holocaust
Italian partisans captureBenito Mussoliniin Dongo, near Lake Como
Babe RuthDay celebrated at Yankee Stadium and throughout the US
Burma PremierU Nu's Volksliga voor Vrijheid loses election
Undefeated world heavyweight boxing championRocky Marcianoretires from the ring
Mao Zedongresigns as Chairman of the PRC after the disastrous failure of the Great Leap Forward
South Korean PresidentSyngman Rheeresigns after 12 years in power
Cuban premierFidel Castroarrives in Moscow
John Lennon's book of poetry and sketches "In His Own Write" is published in US
Dmitri Shostakovichcompletes his 2nd Cello Concert
Curt Floodresigns Senators after 13 games & departs for Denmark
George Harrisonreleases single "Love Comes to Everyone"
Paul McCartney's rock band Wings breaks-up
Nolan Ryanbecomes strikeout king (3,509), passingWalter Johnson
US Justice Department bars Austrian ChancellorKurt Waldheimfrom entering US, due to his aid of Nazi Germany during WW II
DodgerOrel Hershiserundergoes career-threatening shoulder surgery
NY Mets tradeDavid Coneto Toronto Blue Jays for Jeff Kent
Former US PresidentRichard Nixonburied in Nixon Library in Yorba Linda, California

Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at Carnegie Hall, NYC; performers include:Sting,Elton John,James Taylor,Madonna,Billy Joel,Joe Cocker,Emmylou Harris,Roberta Flack,Wynonna Judd, and Tsidii Leloka
U.S. PresidentBarack Obama, coerced by incessant false "birther" accusations, publicly releases a copy of his birth certificate
"Last Week Tonight withJohn Oliver" late-night talk and news show fronted by John Oliver premieres on HBO
Ariana Grande's first single from her album, "Problem" featuring Iggy Azalea, is released
German leaderAngela Merkelhas a 1-day trip to Washington, DC, meeting US PresidentDonald Trump
Historic Korean summit, the North'sKim Jong-unandMoon Jae-inof South Korea agree to officially end Korean war and rid peninsula of nuclear weapons
Swedish bandABBAannounce they have recorded new songs for the first time since 1982
Pope Francisdonates $500,000 for migrants stranded in Mexico trying to reach the US
Brazil's Senate orders an official inquiry into PresidentJair Bolsonaro' and government official's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic
SpaceX, launches its Crew Dragon capsule with four astronauts, including Jessica Watkins - becomes 1st black woman to serve extended mission on International Space Station
Former Vice PresidentMike Penceappears before the federal grand jury investigating PresidentDonald Trump’s efforts to overturn his 2020 election loss
James Corden's final appearance as host of "The Late Late Show" on CBS, after 1,196 episodes
"The Settlers" documentary byLouis Therouxpremiers on BBC2, revisiting Israeli settlers 14 years after the release of his film "The Ultra Zionists"
The Australian Labor Party under Prime Minister Chris Watson becomes the first Labor government in the world
Trial of John Hinckley, Jr begins for the attempted assassination of US PresidentRonald Reagan
Soviet authorities order the evacuation of the city of Pripyat (pop. 50,000) 1 day after the Chernobyl nuclear accident
Global confirmed cases of COVID-19 pass 3 million with the death toll at 205,000. US has 1/3 of all new cases.
